steps.title: Identify what the question asks
steps.content: The question asks for the choice that most effectively emphasizes a difference between Vautier and Whitfield. The notes contrast them: Vautier studied algae in the Canary Islands; Whitfield studied the hills of Northeast Wales. The correct choice must directly contrast these two scientists — their fields or locations must appear side by side with a contrast marker.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Though it was conducted long ago, the research performed by algologist Claire Vautier and geologist Helen Whitfield can help us understand how scientific knowledge evolves.: Groups both scientists together to make a point about historical research — no contrast between them is drawn. ✗
  • Choice B — Algology and geology are just two examples of the myriad fields in which scientists can specialize.: Makes a general point about scientific fields without mentioning either scientist by name. ✗
  • Choice C — Claire Vautier studied algae in the Canary Islands; Helen Whitfield, however, studied the hills of Northeast Wales.: Uses a semicolon and “however” to directly contrast what each scientist studied — exactly what the question asks for. ✓
  • Choice D — Both Claire Vautier and Helen Whitfield are known for their contributions to science.: Emphasizes similarity (“Both”), not a difference. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
